what_it_does: This file is a structured interpretive audit and design-substrate specification for slides 16-30 of a 30-page capital-raise pitch deck. It records the narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, visual findings with recipes and generator rules, reusable components, and content-sample budgets.
observations: The file documents the back half of a pitch deck, shifting from social proof and capital rationale to an evidence-heavy appendix and a full-bleed contact close.
It includes a detailed slide-by-slide attention_flow array describing where the eye is expected to enter, move, and exit.

It contains a findings array with 42 items grouped by category such as gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, color-palette, atom, texture, chrome-mode, zone, sequence, and line-connector.
Each finding has a recipe, evidence, emergent flag, and generator_rule, turning visual observations into prescriptive instructions.
A components section defines 11 reusable UI units such as Person Row, Stat Band, Process Spine, and Contact Card Overlay with reuse notes.
A content_samples section lists 25 text slots with character counts, word counts, and line counts to constrain copy length.
The tone is descriptive and prescriptive at once, mixing visual analysis with brand-system rules.
standouts: A two-tier gold system: data gold (#C8A84B) for figures and names versus title gold (#B8921A) for section labels and emphasis.
A deliberate arrow grammar: right-pointing triangle for forward motion, left-pointing arrow for inbound results, up/down circles for trends.
Two chrome modes: DATA MODE (white grid, full chrome) and GALLERY MODE (full-bleed photo, minimal text) used as narrative gear shifts.
A content-density progression that decreases through the appendix, ending with a sparse contact slide.
QR codes embedded inside architectural photography rather than placed as separate UI elements.
A semi-transparent white contact-card overlay (85-90% opacity) on the final full-bleed image slide.
conventions: Uses a consistent JSON schema with category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent boolean, and generator_rule for every finding.
Labels visual patterns with approximate hex colors, pixel sizes, and slide-scale measurements.
Distinguishes emergent patterns from non-emergent ones without defining the distinction in the file.
Provides reusable components with explicit composition and reuse guidance.
Includes content-sample metadata to enforce line and word budgets.
Maintains a neutral, file-local stance while still making strong interpretive claims about viewer attention and emotional effect.
concerns: Eye-movement and emotional-impact claims are stated as facts without supporting data or methodology.
Color, size, and opacity values are approximate and cannot be verified from the JSON alone.
The emergent flag is used repeatedly but never defined, so its classification basis is unclear.
Some findings blend observation with opinion, e.g., luxury pause signals confidence or reluctance adds credibility.
No source image references or checksums are provided, making the file hard to validate against the original deck.
notes: A dense, prescriptive visual-system ledger for the second half of a capital-raise deck, mixing narrative pacing, attention-flow hypotheses, and generator-ready design rules.
questions: What is the original image or PDF source for these slides?
How is the emergent flag determined for each finding?
What is ConceptV and where is the broader brand system documented?
How does this file relate to part 1 of the same deck?
Were the attention-flow descriptions derived from eye-tracking, expert review, or inference?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an interpretive layer for a ledger of code/design substrates. The actual content is a detailed visual-design audit and generative specification for a pitch-deck appendix. The two align closely: it interprets design choices and encodes them as reusable rules.
apparent_themes: Luxury restraint and spatial breathing room
Systematic visual grammar with semantic color and arrow direction
Warm, gold-dominant minimalism with reserved semantic accents
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to reverse-engineer a finished pitch deck into a reproducible design system, so that the same visual language, narrative rhythm, and component patterns can be generated consistently for related materials.
novelty: The combination of per-slide attention-flow narration, narrative-arc analysis, and generator-rule recipes is unusual; it treats a pitch deck as both a story artifact and a parametric design system.
contribution: This file contributes a rich catalog of reusable design patterns, component definitions, color/type recipes, content budgets, and slide-level attention-flow guidance that a fused system could use to generate or audit pitch-deck artifacts in the same visual language.
summary_for_embedding: A structured interpretive audit and generative design specification for slides 16-30 of a capital-raise pitch deck, covering narrative arc, attention flow, visual findings, reusable components, and content budgets.
intention_for_embedding: To encode the visual language, narrative pacing, and component grammar of a pitch-deck appendix into reusable, prescriptive design rules.
